message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #4 ready for review, need 9/9">
Task #4 (HTMX empty state transition) committed and pushed (4e049aa). CI is running.

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review and send consent or concerns. We need 9/9 consensus.

Note: Carson pushed before receiving the process update — that's fine. The new flow (consensus before push) applies starting with Task #5.
</teammate-message>

content.content:
commit 4e049aa51c00ac6e0983793d1fd091ee7ab9191c
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:37:58 2026 -0800

    Add HTMX empty state transition when last todo is deleted
    
    The empty state message ("No todos yet.") is now always present in the
    DOM with id="todo-empty", hidden via the hidden attribute when items
    exist. The todo-focus.js script toggles visibility after HTMX delete
    and add swaps, so the empty state appears when the last item is
    deleted and disappears when the first item is added.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 static/js/todo-focus.js | 53 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++----
 templates/todos.html    |  4 +---
 tests/api/todos.rs      | 21 ++++++++++++++++----
 3 files changed, 67 insertions(+), 11 deletions(-)

commit 4e049aa51c00ac6e0983793d1fd091ee7ab9191c
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:37:58 2026 -0800

    Add HTMX empty state transition when last todo is deleted
    
    The empty state message ("No todos yet.") is now always present in the
    DOM with id="todo-empty", hidden via the hidden attribute when items
    exist. The todo-focus.js script toggles visibility after HTMX delete
    and add swaps, so the empty state appears when the last item is
    deleted and disappears when the first item is added.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 static/js/todo-focus.js | 53 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++----
 templates/todos.html    |  4 +---
 tests/api/todos.rs      | 21 ++++++++++++++++----
 3 files changed, 67 insertions(+), 11 deletions(-)

input.description: View full diff for Task #4 commit

content.text: Thorough review complete. Let me send my assessment.

input.content:
**Review of 4e049aa -- Kent Beck (TDD Coach)** **I consent.** **What changed:** 1. `static/js/todo-focus.js` -- Extended with empty state toggle logic. New `updateEmptyState()` function checks if `.todo-list` has any `.todo-item` children and toggles the `hidden` attribute on both the list and the `#todo-empty` message. Called after deletes (via `htmx:afterSettle`) and after adds (via `htmx:afterRequest`). 2. `templates/todos.html` -- The empty state `

` is now always in the DOM with `id="todo-empty"`, using the `hidden` attribute instead of `{% if %}` conditional rendering. This is the key architectural change. 3. `tests/api/todos.rs` -- Updated existing tests to verify the new structure: empty state element always present in DOM, `hidden` attribute present when items exist, absent when no items. **TDD assessment -- good improvements here:** - The *existing* tests were updated to match the new DOM structure. The empty-list test now verifies `id="todo-empty"` exists and is NOT hidden. The has-items test now verifies `id="todo-empty"` exists and IS hidden. This is better than the old tests which just checked for the presence/absence of the text -- now they verify the toggle mechanism. - Tests cover both states: empty list (visible message, hidden list) and populated list (hidden message, visible list). - The test assertions on `id="todo-empty" hidden` vs `id="todo-empty"` without hidden are precise and correct. **Design quality:** 1. **Always-in-DOM pattern is correct.** Moving from conditional Askama rendering (`{% if %}`) to always-in-DOM with `hidden` attribute is the right call. The previous approach required the server to re-render the full page to show/hide the empty state. Now JS can toggle it client-side. This matches our a11y pattern of keeping elements in the DOM for ARIA targets. 2. **`updateEmptyState()` is a clean extracted function.** It's called from two places (after delete, after add) and is side-effect-free beyond the DOM manipulation. The guard clause (`if (!todoList || !emptyMsg) return`) handles the case where elements are missing gracefully. 3. **Both directions are handled:** delete last item shows empty state, add first item hides empty state. Symmetric state transitions -- no way to get stuck in the wrong state. 4. **The `htmx:afterRequest` listener for adds** correctly checks `elt.classList.contains("todo-add")` and `e.detail.successful` before calling `updateEmptyState()`. This prevents the empty state from toggling on failed add attempts (e.g., validation errors). 5. **The `pendingDeleteSwap` flag** cleanly separates the delete-specific empty state check from the general focus management. No confusion about when to update. **One observation (non-blocking):** The old test asserted `!body.contains("No todos yet.")` when items exist. The new test asserts `body.contains("id=\"todo-empty\" hidden")`. The new assertion is strictly better -- it verifies the mechanism, not just the visible text. Nice upgrade. Clean, well-structured change. Good work, Carson.

content.text: Consent sent for Task #4. One more task to go -- Task #5 (HTMX inline editing) will be the most complex of Carson's set. Standing by.
